html:
Asia Precision (APCS-R.BK): THB 1.42

Asia Precision is not a THB-priced stock with a USD 937 million market cap. The latest SET factsheet shows 660 million paid-up shares, a THB 937.2 million market cap, THB 1.416 billion EV, and 0.83x P/BV at THB 1.42. That data correction matters because the stock is being screened globally as either irrelevant, loss-making, or incorrectly scaled. The real setup is a small Thai special situation: a failed energy/EPC/bitcoin-mining pivot that may now be close to exhausting the losses that made trailing numbers unusable.

This is not a solar-asset FCFE story. The official business mix is EPC/O&M for alternative energy and utilities, raw water distribution, holding-company investments, and digital assets. The forward thesis is narrower but more credible: 2025 and Q1 2026 were dominated by two ugly but fixable drags, bitcoin mining and loss-making EPC contracts. If management actually scales down mining and the completed EPC problem contracts stop bleeding, APCS can move from a screen-rejected net loss to a low-capex, asset-backed turnaround trading below parent-level book value.

The FY2025 numbers were awful on the surface: THB 916 million revenue, THB 478.5 million operating loss, and THB 498.3 million loss attributable to owners. But the segment detail is the point. Holding-company operations made THB 40.7 million operating profit. Water was slightly profitable. The damage came from EPC at negative THB 203.7 million and bitcoin mining at negative THB 318.0 million, including THB 208.3 million of mining impairment and fair-value adjustments. Management said the loss-making infrastructure and utility projects were completed and delivered by Q3 2025 and that their losses had already been recognized, with no further burden expected. That is the first real inflection claim.

Q1 2026 was still a loss, but the mix improved underneath. Revenue fell 46% YoY to THB 148.6 million and net loss widened to THB 86.3 million, yet EPC operating loss improved from THB 44.9 million to THB 23.9 million. The raw water business flipped to THB 3.7 million operating profit despite lower revenue, helped by better reserve and cost management. The biomass plant managed by a subsidiary resumed normal operations in March 2026, which should reduce idle fixed-cost leakage in later quarters. The continuing problem is mining, where revenue collapsed 89% YoY, the segment lost THB 57.4 million, and management disclosed it is reviewing plans to scale down or temporarily suspend operations because economics remain unfavorable.

That last sentence is the investment hinge. APCS should not be valued as a bitcoin proxy. The mining business is not showing torque to Bitcoin because difficulty, power cost, depreciation, and hardware obsolescence are absorbing the upside. The bull case is not “Bitcoin goes up.” The bull case is “management stops feeding capital and electricity into an uneconomic mining segment, crystallizes the impairment already taken, and lets the residual holding, water, and EPC/O&M platform show through.”

At THB 1.42, the market is paying THB 937 million for THB 1.138 billion of consolidated equity and THB 1.685 billion of separate parent equity. The discount is deserved while losses continue, but it is not hopeless. Separate equity barely moved in Q1 2026, while consolidated equity fell because subsidiaries absorbed the digital and EPC losses. Liabilities are not trivial, but they are moving in the right direction: FY2025 consolidated liabilities fell 15.6% YoY after scheduled debenture repayment, and Q1 separate liabilities fell another THB 30 million as the company repaid debentures and bank loans.

The variant perception is that APCS is labeled three different wrong ways. Old databases still describe it as a precision metal-parts manufacturer. Thai retail news summarizes it as a widening Q1 loss. Crypto-sensitive investors see a bitcoin miner with broken economics. The better label is a sub-USD-30 million Thai holding-company turnaround with a shrinking loss pool, a modest profitable utility asset, and a management decision pending on whether to stop the mining drain. NVDR ownership is only 0.53% and foreign ownership is under 10%, so there is almost no foreign sponsorship today.

I would not pay for blue-sky data center optionality unless management announces a real power, site, or customer contract. ASIC mining hardware is not a clean AI compute asset. But the company does have an energy-heavy operating footprint and utility/EPC relationships, so a disciplined pivot toward lower-risk energy services, O&M, water, or power-backed infrastructure would be credible. The stock does not need that to work. It only needs mining losses to stop and EPC to move from negative THB 200 million toward breakeven.

A reasonable 2027 base case is holding profit around THB 30 to 40 million, water profit around THB 15 to 25 million, EPC near breakeven to modestly profitable, mining losses reduced to near zero after suspension or deep curtailment, and finance cost around THB 35 to 45 million. That produces only THB 20 to 60 million net income, so APCS is not optically cheap on clean earnings yet. The valuation support comes from book value and from the earnings delta: moving from a THB 498 million loss to even small profitability is a major perception change for a THB 937 million market cap.

The main risk is that management does not stop the value leakage. If mining continues because of sunk-cost logic, or if new EPC contracts are priced badly to win revenue, APCS can keep consuming book value. Governance and capital allocation are therefore the whole story. A rights issue, fresh debt for speculative digital assets, or another year of subsidiary losses would break the thesis.

The best catalyst is simple: Q2 and Q3 2026 filings showing mining curtailed, EPC losses narrowing, and no new dilution. A second catalyst would be any sale, write-down completion, or redeployment of mining assets that lowers depreciation and power burn. A third would be water or EPC backlog disclosure that proves the remaining platform can earn money without crypto help.

Bear case: THB 0.85, down 40%. Mining remains active and loss-making, EPC does not normalize, consolidated equity falls below THB 1.0 billion, and the stock trades near 0.55x to 0.60x impaired book.

Base case: THB 2.05, up 44%. Mining is scaled down, EPC losses fade, water stays profitable, and the market values APCS around 0.8x parent book or roughly 1.2x consolidated book as the loss narrative ends.

Bull case: THB 3.20, up 125%. APCS exits or deeply curtails mining, EPC/O&M returns to THB 50 to 80 million operating profit, water contributes steadily, no dilution occurs, and investors re-rate the company toward 1.2x parent book plus a modest value for restored earnings power.
